Attribute name prefix.name: Attribute name prefix
Attribute name prefix.description: The prefix to add to the generated attribute names. For example, if this is set to 'rolling.window.', then the full attribute names will be 'rolling.window.value', 'rolling.window.count', etc.

Time window.name: Time window
Time window.description: The amount of time for a rolling window. The format of the value is expected to be a count followed by a time unit. For example 5 millis, 10 secs, 1 min, 3 hours, 2 days, etc.

Value to track.name: Value to track
Value to track.description: The expression on which to evaluate each FlowFile. The result of the expression will be added to the rolling window value.

Window length.name: Window length
Window length.description: The window length in number of values. Takes precedence over 'Time window'. If set to zero, the 'Time window' property is used instead.

supportedRelationships.name: failure
supportedRelationships.description: When a FlowFile fails, it is routed here.

Attributes List.name: Attributes List
Attributes List.description: Comma separated list of attributes to be included in the resulting JSON. If this value is left empty then all existing Attributes will be included. This list of attributes is case sensitive. If an attribute specified in the list is not found it will be be emitted to the resulting JSON with an empty string or NULL value.

Attributes Regular Expression.name: Attributes Regular Expression
Attributes Regular Expression.description: Regular expression that will be evaluated against the flow file attributes to select the matching attributes. Both the matching attributes and the selected attributes from the Attributes List property will be written in the resulting JSON.

Destination.name: Destination
Destination.description: Control if JSON value is written as a new flowfile attribute 'JSONAttributes' or written in the flowfile content. Writing to flowfile content will overwrite any existing flowfile content.

Include Core Attributes.name: Include Core Attributes
Include Core Attributes.description: Determines if the FlowFile core attributes which are contained in every FlowFile should be included in the final JSON value generated.

Null Value.name: Null Value
Null Value.description: If true a non existing selected attribute will be NULL in the resulting JSON. If false an empty string will be placed in the JSON.

Kubernetes Controller Service.description: Controller service which provides Kubernetes functionality

Batch Size.name: Batch Size
Batch Size.description: Maximum number of FlowFiles processed in a single session

Compression Level.name: Compression Level
Compression Level.description: The compression level to use; this is valid only when using GZIP compression.

Encapsulate in TAR.name: Encapsulate in TAR
Encapsulate in TAR.description:
If true, on compression the FlowFile is added to a TAR archive and then compressed, and on decompression a compressed, TAR-encapsulated FlowFile is expected.
If false, on compression the content of the FlowFile simply gets compressed, and on decompression a simple compressed content is expected.
true is the behaviour compatible with older MiNiFi C++ versions, false is the behaviour compatible with NiFi.

Mode.name: Mode
Mode.description: Indicates whether the processor should compress content or decompress content.

Update Filename.name: Update Filename
Update Filename.description: Determines if filename extension need to be updated

supportedRelationships.name: failure
supportedRelationships.description: FlowFiles will be transferred to the failure relationship if they fail to compress/decompress

Batch Size.description: The maximum number of entries processed in a single execution.

Include Timestamp.name: Include Timestamp
Include Timestamp.description: Include message timestamp in the 'timestamp' attribute.

Payload Format.name: Payload Format
Payload Format.description: Configures flow file content formatting. Raw: only the message. Syslog: similar to syslog or journalctl output.

Process Old Messages.name: Process Old Messages
Process Old Messages.description: Process events created before the first usage (schedule) of the processor instance.

Timestamp Format.name: Timestamp Format
Timestamp Format.description: Format string to use when creating the timestamp attribute or writing messages in the syslog format. ISO/ISO 8601/ISO8601 are equivalent to "%FT%T%Ez". See https://howardhinnant.github.io/date/date.html#to_stream_formatting for all flags.

supportedRelationships.name: success
supportedRelationships.description: Successfully consumed journal messages.

processors.typeDescription: Consume systemd-journald journal messages. Creates one flow file per message. Fields are mapped to attributes. Realtime timestamp is mapped to the 'timestamp' attribute. Available on Linux only.

Commit Offsets Policy.name: Commit Offsets Policy
Commit Offsets Policy.description: NoCommit disables offset commiting entirely. AutoCommit configures Kafka to automatically increase offsets after serving the messages. CommitAfterBatch commits offsets after the messages has been converted to flowfiles. CommitFromIncomingFlowFiles consumes incoming flowfiles and commits the offsets based on their attributes.

Duplicate Header Handling.name: Duplicate Header Handling
Duplicate Header Handling.description:
For headers to be added as attributes, this option specifies how to handle cases where multiple headers are present with the same key. For example in case of receiving these two headers: "Accept: text/html" and "Accept: application/xml" and we want to attach the value of "Accept" as a FlowFile attribute:
 - "Keep First" attaches: "Accept -> text/html"
 - "Keep Latest" attaches: "Accept -> application/xml"
 - "Comma-separated Merge" attaches: "Accept -> text/html, application/xml"

Group ID.name: Group ID
Group ID.description: A Group ID is used to identify consumers that are within the same consumer group. Corresponds to Kafka's 'group.id' property.

Headers To Add As Attributes.name: Headers To Add As Attributes
Headers To Add As Attributes.description: A comma separated list to match against all message headers. Any message header whose name matches an item from the list will be added to the FlowFile as an Attribute. If not specified, no Header values will be added as FlowFile attributes. The behaviour on when multiple headers of the same name are present is set using the Duplicate Header Handling attribute.

Honor Transactions.name: Honor Transactions
Honor Transactions.description: Specifies whether or not MiNiFi should honor transactional guarantees when communicating with Kafka. If false, the Processor will use an "isolation level" of read_uncomitted. This means that messages will be received as soon as they are written to Kafka but will be pulled, even if the producer cancels the transactions. If this value is true, MiNiFi will not receive any messages for which the producer's transaction was canceled, but this can result in some latency since the consumer must wait for the producer to finish its entire transaction instead of pulling as the messages become available.

Kafka Brokers.name: Kafka Brokers
Kafka Brokers.description: A comma-separated list of known Kafka Brokers in the format <host>:<port>.

Kerberos Keytab Path.name: Kerberos Keytab Path
Kerberos Keytab Path.description: The path to the location on the local filesystem where the kerberos keytab is located. Read permission on the file is required.

Key Attribute Encoding.name: Key Attribute Encoding
Key Attribute Encoding.description: FlowFiles that are emitted have an attribute named 'kafka.key'. This property dictates how the value of the attribute should be encoded.

Max Poll Records.name: Max Poll Records
Max Poll Records.description: Specifies the maximum number of records Kafka should return when polling each time the processor is triggered.

Max Poll Time.name: Max Poll Time
Max Poll Time.description: Specifies the maximum amount of time the consumer can use for polling data from the brokers. Polling is a blocking operation, so the upper limit of this value is specified in 4 seconds.

Message Demarcator.name: Message Demarcator
Message Demarcator.description: Since KafkaConsumer receives messages in batches, you have an option to output FlowFiles which contains all Kafka messages in a single batch for a given topic and partition and this property allows you to provide a string (interpreted as UTF-8) to use for demarcating apart multiple Kafka messages. This is an optional property and if not provided each Kafka message received will result in a single FlowFile which time it is triggered.

Message Header Encoding.name: Message Header Encoding
Message Header Encoding.description: Any message header that is found on a Kafka message will be added to the outbound FlowFile as an attribute. This property indicates the Character Encoding to use for deserializing the headers.

Offset Reset.name: Offset Reset
Offset Reset.description: Allows you to manage the condition when there is no initial offset in Kafka or if the current offset does not exist any more on the server (e.g. because that data has been deleted). Corresponds to Kafka's 'auto.offset.reset' property.

Password.name: Password
Password.description: The password for the given username when the SASL Mechanism is sasl_plaintext

SASL Mechanism.name: SASL Mechanism
SASL Mechanism.description: The SASL mechanism to use for authentication. Corresponds to Kafka's 'sasl.mechanism' property.

Security Protocol.name: Security Protocol
Security Protocol.description: Protocol used to communicate with brokers. Corresponds to Kafka's 'security.protocol' property.

Session Timeout.name: Session Timeout
Session Timeout.description: Client group session and failure detection timeout. The consumer sends periodic heartbeats to indicate its liveness to the broker. If no hearts are received by the broker for a group member within the session timeout, the broker will remove the consumer from the group and trigger a rebalance. The allowed range is configured with the broker configuration properties group.min.session.timeout.ms and group.max.session.timeout.ms.

Topic Name Format.name: Topic Name Format
Topic Name Format.description: Specifies whether the Topic(s) provided are a comma separated list of names or a single regular expression. Using regular expressions does not automatically discover Kafka topics created after the processor started.

Topic Names.name: Topic Names
Topic Names.description: The name of the Kafka Topic(s) to pull from. Multiple topic names are supported as a comma separated list.

Username.name: Username
Username.description: The username when the SASL Mechanism is sasl_plaintext

supportedRelationships.name: success
supportedRelationships.description: Incoming Kafka messages as flowfiles. Depending on the demarcation strategy, this can be one or multiple messages per flowfile.

The application should make sure that the processor is triggered at regular intervals, even if no messages are expected, to serve any queued callbacks waiting to be called. Add Attributes As Fields.name: Add Attributes As Fields
Add Attributes As Fields.description: If setting this property to true, default fields are going to be added in each record: _topic, _qos, _isDuplicate, _isRetained.

Attribute From Content Type.name: Attribute From Content Type
Attribute From Content Type.description: Name of FlowFile attribute to be filled from content type of received message. MQTT 5.x only.

Broker URI.name: Broker URI
Broker URI.description: The URI to use to connect to the MQTT broker

Clean Session.name: Clean Session
Clean Session.description: Whether to start afresh rather than remembering previous subscriptions. If true, then make broker forget subscriptions after disconnected. MQTT 3.x only.

Clean Start.name: Clean Start
Clean Start.description: Whether to start afresh rather than remembering previous subscriptions. MQTT 5.x only.

Client ID.name: Client ID
Client ID.description: MQTT client ID to use. If not set, a UUID will be generated.

Connection Timeout.name: Connection Timeout
Connection Timeout.description: Maximum time interval the client will wait for the network connection to the MQTT broker

Keep Alive Interval.name: Keep Alive Interval
Keep Alive Interval.description: Defines the maximum time interval between messages sent or received

Last Will Content Type.name: Last Will Content Type
Last Will Content Type.description: Content type of the client's Last Will. MQTT 5.x only.

Last Will Message.name: Last Will Message
Last Will Message.description: The message to send as the client's Last Will. If the Last Will Message is empty, Last Will will be deleted from the broker

Last Will QoS.name: Last Will QoS
Last Will QoS.description: The Quality of Service (QoS) to send the last will with.

Last Will Retain.name: Last Will Retain
Last Will Retain.description: Whether to retain the client's Last Will

Last Will Topic.name: Last Will Topic
Last Will Topic.description: The topic to send the client's Last Will to. If the Last Will topic is not set then a Last Will will not be sent

MQTT Version.name: MQTT Version
MQTT Version.description: The MQTT specification version when connecting to the broker.

Password.name: Password
Password.description: Password to use when connecting to the broker

Quality of Service.name: Quality of Service
Quality of Service.description: The Quality of Service (QoS) of messages.

Queue Max Message.name: Queue Max Message
Queue Max Message.description: Maximum number of messages allowed on the received MQTT queue

Receive Maximum.name: Receive Maximum
Receive Maximum.description: Maximum number of unacknowledged messages allowed. MQTT 5.x only.

Record Reader.name: Record Reader
Record Reader.description: The Record Reader to use for parsing received MQTT Messages into Records.

Record Writer.name: Record Writer
Record Writer.description: The Record Writer to use for serializing Records before writing them to a FlowFile.

Security CA.name: Security CA
Security CA.description: File or directory path to CA certificate(s) for verifying the broker's key

Security Cert.name: Security Cert
Security Cert.description: Path to client's public key (PEM) used for authentication

Security Private Key.name: Security Private Key
Security Private Key.description: Path to client's private key (PEM) used for authentication

Security Protocol.name: Security Protocol
Security Protocol.description: Protocol used to communicate with brokers

Session Expiry Interval.name: Session Expiry Interval
Session Expiry Interval.description: Time to delete session on broker after client is disconnected. MQTT 5.x only.

Topic Alias Maximum.name: Topic Alias Maximum
Topic Alias Maximum.description: Maximum number of topic aliases to use. If set to 0, then topic aliases cannot be used. MQTT 5.x only.

Username.description: Username to use when connecting to the broker

Include Zero Record FlowFiles.description: When converting an incoming FlowFile, if the conversion results in no data, this property specifies whether or not a FlowFile will be sent to the corresponding relationship.

Blob.description: The filename of the blob. If left empty the filename attribute will be used by default.

Common Storage Account Endpoint Suffix.name: Common Storage Account Endpoint Suffix
Common Storage Account Endpoint Suffix.description: Storage accounts in public Azure always use a common FQDN suffix. Override this endpoint suffix with a different suffix in certain circumstances (like Azure Stack or non-public Azure regions).

Connection String.description: Connection string used to connect to Azure Storage service. This overrides all other set credential properties if Credential Configuration Strategy is set to From Properties.

Container Name.description: Name of the Azure Storage container. In case of PutAzureBlobStorage processor, container can be created if it does not exist.

Credential Configuration Strategy.name: Credential Configuration Strategy
Credential Configuration Strategy.description: The strategy to use for credential configuration. If set to From Properties, the credentials are parsed from the SAS Token, Storage Account Key, and Connection String properties. In other cases, the selected Azure identity source is used.

Delete Snapshots Option.description: Specifies the snapshot deletion options to be used when deleting a blob. None: Deletes the blob only. Include Snapshots: Delete the blob and its snapshots. Delete Snapshots Only: Delete only the blob's snapshots.

Managed Identity Client ID.name: Managed Identity Client ID
Managed Identity Client ID.description: Client ID of the managed identity. The property is required when User Assigned Managed Identity is used for authentication and multiple user-assigned identities are added to the resource. It must be empty in case of System Assigned Managed Identity and can also be left empty if only one user-assigned identity is present.

Proxy Configuration Service.description: Specifies the Proxy Configuration Controller Service to proxy network requests.

SAS Token.name: SAS Token
SAS Token.description: Shared Access Signature token. Specify either SAS Token (recommended) or Storage Account Key together with Storage Account Name if Credential Configuration Strategy is set to From Properties. If set, Storage Account Key must be empty.

Storage Account Key.name: Storage Account Key
Storage Account Key.description: The storage account key. This is an admin-like password providing access to every container in this account. It is recommended one uses Shared Access Signature (SAS) token instead for fine-grained control with policies if Credential Configuration Strategy is set to From Properties. If set, SAS Token must be empty.

Server Side Encryption Key.description: The AES256 Encryption Key (encoded in base64) for server-side decryption of the object.

AWS Credentials Provider service.description: The name of the AWS Credentials Provider controller service that is used to obtain AWS credentials.

Communications Timeout.description: Sets the timeout of the communication between the AWS server and the client

Credentials File.description: Path to a file containing AWS access key and secret key in properties file format. Properties used: accessKey and secretKey

Endpoint Override URL.name: Endpoint Override URL
Endpoint Override URL.description: Endpoint URL to use instead of the AWS default including scheme, host, port, and path. The AWS libraries select an endpoint URL based on the AWS region, but this property overrides the selected endpoint URL, allowing use with other S3-compatible endpoints.

Object Key.name: Object Key
Object Key.description: The key of the S3 object. If none is given the filename attribute will be used by default.

Proxy Configuration Service.name: Proxy Configuration Service
Proxy Configuration Service.description: Specifies the Proxy Configuration Controller Service to proxy network requests. When used, this will override any values specified for Proxy Host, Proxy Port, Proxy Username, and Proxy Password properties.

Proxy Host.name: Proxy Host
Proxy Host.description: Proxy host name or IP. Use https:// prefix for HTTPS proxy. DEPRECATED, please use Proxy Configuration Service instead.

Proxy Password.name: Proxy Password
Proxy Password.description: Password to set when authenticating against proxy. DEPRECATED, please use Proxy Configuration Service instead.

Proxy Port.name: Proxy Port
Proxy Port.description: The port number of the proxy host. DEPRECATED, please use Proxy Configuration Service instead.

Proxy Username.name: Proxy Username
Proxy Username.description: Username to set when authenticating against proxy. DEPRECATED, please use Proxy Configuration Service instead.

Use Default Credentials.name: Use Default Credentials
Use Default Credentials.description: If true, uses the Default Credential chain, including EC2 instance profiles or roles, environment variables, default user credentials, etc.

Version.name: Version
Version.description: The Version of the Object to delete

Null Value Representation.name: Null Value Representation
Null Value Representation.description: Indicates the desired representation of JSON Path expressions resulting in a null value.

Path Not Found Behavior.name: Path Not Found Behavior
Path Not Found Behavior.description: Indicates how to handle missing JSON path expressions when destination is set to 'flowfile-attribute'. Selecting 'warn' will generate a warning when a JSON path expression is not found. Selecting 'skip' will omit attributes for any unmatched JSON path expressions.

Return Type.name: Return Type

supportedRelationships.name: unmatched
supportedRelationships.description: FlowFiles are routed to this relationship when the JsonPath does not match the content of the FlowFile and the Destination is set to flowfile-content

processors.typeDescription: Evaluates one or more JsonPath expressions against the content of a FlowFile. The results of those expressions are assigned to FlowFile Attributes or are written to the content of the FlowFile itself, depending on configuration of the Processor. JsonPaths are entered by adding user-defined properties; the name of the property maps to the Attribute Name into which the result will be placed (if the Destination is flowfile-attribute; otherwise, the property name is ignored). The value of the property must be a valid JsonPath expression. A Return Type of 'auto-detect' will make a determination based off the configured destination. When 'Destination' is set to 'flowfile-attribute,' a return type of 'scalar' will be used. When 'Destination' is set to 'flowfile-content,' a return type of 'JSON' will be used.If the JsonPath evaluates to a JSON array or JSON object and the Return Type is set to 'scalar' the FlowFile will be unmodified and will be routed to failure. A Return Type of JSON can return scalar values if the provided JsonPath evaluates to the specified value and will be routed as a match.If Destination is 'flowfile-content' and the JsonPath does not evaluate to a defined path, the FlowFile will be routed to 'unmatched' without having its contents modified. If Destination is 'flowfile-attribute' and the expression matches nothing, attributes will be created with empty strings as the value unless 'Path Not Found Behaviour' is set to 'skip', and the FlowFile will always be routed to 'matched.'

Batch Duration.name: Batch Duration
Batch Duration.description: If the process is expected to be long-running and produce textual output, a batch duration can be specified.

Command.name: Command
Command.description: Specifies the command to be executed; if just the name of an executable is provided, it must be in the user's environment PATH.

Command Arguments.name: Command Arguments
Command Arguments.description: The arguments to supply to the executable delimited by white space. White space can be escaped by enclosing it in double-quotes.

Redirect Error Stream.name: Redirect Error Stream
Redirect Error Stream.description: If true will redirect any error stream output of the process to the output stream.

Working Directory.name: Working Directory

When this option is used, the output is expected to be in textual format, as it typically does not make sense to split binary data on arbitrary time-based intervals. Script Body.description: Body of script to execute. Only one of Script File or Script Body may be used

Script Engine.description: The engine to execute scripts (python, lua)

Script File.description: Path to script file to execute. Only one of Script File or Script Body may be used

processors.typeDescription: Executes a script given the flow file and a process session. The script is responsible for handling the incoming flow file (transfer to SUCCESS or remove, e.g.) as well as any flow files created by the script. If the handling is incomplete or incorrect, the session will be rolled back.Scripts must define an onTrigger function which accepts NiFi Context and Property objects. For efficiency, scripts are executed once when the processor is run, then the onTrigger method is called for each incoming flowfile. This enables scripts to keep state if they wish, although there will be a script context per concurrent task of the processor. In order to, e.g., compute an arithmetic sum based on incoming flow file information, set the concurrent tasks to 1.

Max Rows Per Flow File.name: Max Rows Per Flow File
Max Rows Per Flow File.description: The maximum number of result rows that will be included in a single FlowFile. This will allow you to break up very large result sets into multiple FlowFiles. If the value specified is zero, then all rows are returned in a single FlowFile.

SQL select query.name: SQL select query
SQL select query.description: The SQL select query to execute. The query can be empty, a constant value, or built from attributes using Expression Language. If this property is specified, it will be used regardless of the content of incoming flowfiles. If this property is empty, the content of the incoming flow file is expected to contain a valid SQL select query, to be issued by the processor to the database. Note that Expression Language is not evaluated for flow file contents.

supportedRelationships.name: failure
supportedRelationships.description: Flow files containing malformed sql statements

supportedRelationships.name: success
supportedRelationships.description: Successfully created FlowFile from SQL query result set.

processors.typeDescription: Execute provided SQL query. Query result rows will be outputted as new flow files with attribute keys equal to result column names and values equal to result values. There will be one output FlowFile per result row. This processor can be scheduled to run using the standard timer-based scheduling methods, or it can be triggered by an incoming FlowFile. If it is triggered by an incoming FlowFile, then attributes of that FlowFile will be available when evaluating the query.

Enable Case-insensitive Matching.name: Enable Case-insensitive Matching
Enable Case-insensitive Matching.description: Indicates that two characters match even if they are in a different case.

Enable repeating capture group.name: Enable repeating capture group
Enable repeating capture group.description: f set to true, every string matching the capture groups will be extracted. Otherwise, if the Regular Expression matches more than once, only the first match will be extracted.

Include Capture Group 0.name: Include Capture Group 0
Include Capture Group 0.description: Indicates that Capture Group 0 should be included as an attribute. Capture Group 0 represents the entirety of the regular expression match, is typically not used, and could have considerable length.

Maximum Capture Group Length.name: Maximum Capture Group Length
Maximum Capture Group Length.description: Specifies the maximum number of characters a given capture group value can have. Any characters beyond the max will be truncated.

Regex Mode.name: Regex Mode
Regex Mode.description: Set this to extract parts of flowfile content using regular experssions in dynamic properties

Size Limit.name: Size Limit
Size Limit.description: Maximum number of bytes to read into the attribute. 0 for no limit. Default is 2MB.

supportedRelationships.name: success
supportedRelationships.description: success operational on the flow record

processors.typeDescription: Extracts the content of a FlowFile and places it into an attribute.

Azure Storage Credentials Service.name: Azure Storage Credentials Service
Azure Storage Credentials Service.description: Name of the Azure Storage Credentials Service used to retrieve the connection string from.

Blob.name: Blob
Blob.description: The filename of the blob. If left empty the filename attribute will be used by default.

Common Storage Account Endpoint Suffix.name: Common Storage Account Endpoint Suffix
Common Storage Account Endpoint Suffix.description: Storage accounts in public Azure always use a common FQDN suffix. Override this endpoint suffix with a different suffix in certain circumstances (like Azure Stack or non-public Azure regions).

Connection String.name: Connection String
Connection String.description: Connection string used to connect to Azure Storage service. This overrides all other set credential properties if Credential Configuration Strategy is set to From Properties.

Container Name.name: Container Name
Container Name.description: Name of the Azure Storage container. In case of PutAzureBlobStorage processor, container can be created if it does not exist.

Credential Configuration Strategy.name: Credential Configuration Strategy
Credential Configuration Strategy.description: The strategy to use for credential configuration. If set to From Properties, the credentials are parsed from the SAS Token, Storage Account Key, and Connection String properties. In other cases, the selected Azure identity source is used.

Managed Identity Client ID.name: Managed Identity Client ID
Managed Identity Client ID.description: Client ID of the managed identity. The property is required when User Assigned Managed Identity is used for authentication and multiple user-assigned identities are added to the resource. It must be empty in case of System Assigned Managed Identity and can also be left empty if only one user-assigned identity is present.

Proxy Configuration Service.name: Proxy Configuration Service
Proxy Configuration Service.description: Specifies the Proxy Configuration Controller Service to proxy network requests.

Range Length.name: Range Length
Range Length.description: The number of bytes to download from the blob, starting from the Range Start. An empty value or a value that extends beyond the end of the blob will read to the end of the blob.

Range Start.name: Range Start
Range Start.description: The byte position at which to start reading from the blob. An empty value or a value of zero will start reading at the beginning of the blob.

SAS Token.name: SAS Token
SAS Token.description: Shared Access Signature token. Specify either SAS Token (recommended) or Storage Account Key together with Storage Account Name if Credential Configuration Strategy is set to From Properties. If set, Storage Account Key must be empty.

Storage Account Key.name: Storage Account Key
Storage Account Key.description: The storage account key. This is an admin-like password providing access to every container in this account. It is recommended one uses Shared Access Signature (SAS) token instead for fine-grained control with policies if Credential Configuration Strategy is set to From Properties. If set, SAS Token must be empty.

supportedRelationships.name: failure
supportedRelationships.description: Unsuccessful operations will be transferred to the failure relationship

supportedRelationships.name: success
supportedRelationships.description: All successfully processed FlowFiles are routed to this relationship

processors.typeDescription: Retrieves contents of an Azure Storage Blob, writing the contents to the content of the FlowFile

Azure Storage Credentials Service.name: Azure Storage Credentials Service
Azure Storage Credentials Service.description: Name of the Azure Storage Credentials Service used to retrieve the connection string from.

Directory Name.name: Directory Name
Directory Name.description: Name of the Azure Storage Directory. The Directory Name cannot contain a leading '/'. If left empty it designates the root directory. The directory will be created if not already existing.

File Name.name: File Name
File Name.description: The filename in Azure Storage. If left empty the filename attribute will be used by default.

Filesystem Name.name: Filesystem Name
Filesystem Name.description: Name of the Azure Storage File System. It is assumed to be already existing.

Number of Retries.name: Number of Retries
Number of Retries.description: The number of automatic retries to perform if the download fails.

Proxy Configuration Service.name: Proxy Configuration Service
Proxy Configuration Service.description: Specifies the Proxy Configuration Controller Service to proxy network requests.

Range Length.name: Range Length
Range Length.description: The number of bytes to download from the object, starting from the Range Start. An empty value or a value that extends beyond the end of the object will read to the end of the object.

Range Start.name: Range Start
Range Start.description: The byte position at which to start reading from the object. An empty value or a value of zero will start reading at the beginning of the object.

supportedRelationships.name: failure
supportedRelationships.description: In case of fetch failure flowfiles are transferred to this relationship

supportedRelationships.name: success
supportedRelationships.description: Files that have been successfully fetched from Azure storage are transferred to this relationship

processors.typeDescription: Fetch the provided file from Azure Data Lake Storage Gen 2

Completion Strategy.name: Completion Strategy
Completion Strategy.description: Specifies what to do with the original file on the file system once it has been pulled into MiNiFi

File to Fetch.name: File to Fetch
File to Fetch.description: The fully-qualified filename of the file to fetch from the file system. If not defined the default ${absolute.path}/${filename} path is used.

Log level when file not found.name: Log level when file not found
Log level when file not found.description: Log level to use in case the file does not exist when the processor is triggered

Log level when permission denied.name: Log level when permission denied
Log level when permission denied.description: Log level to use in case agent does not have sufficient permissions to read the file

Move Conflict Strategy.name: Move Conflict Strategy
Move Conflict Strategy.description: If Completion Strategy is set to Move File and a file already exists in the destination directory with the same name, this property specifies how that naming conflict should be resolved

Move Destination Directory.name: Move Destination Directory
Move Destination Directory.description: The directory to move the original file to once it has been fetched from the file system. This property is ignored unless the Completion Strategy is set to "Move File". If the directory does not exist, it will be created.

supportedRelationships.name: failure
supportedRelationships.description: Any FlowFile that could not be fetched from the file system for any reason other than insufficient permissions or the file not existing will be transferred to this Relationship.

supportedRelationships.name: not.found
supportedRelationships.description: Any FlowFile that could not be fetched from the file system because the file could not be found will be transferred to this Relationship.

supportedRelationships.name: permission.denied
supportedRelationships.description: Any FlowFile that could not be fetched from the file system due to the user running MiNiFi not having sufficient permissions will be transferred to this Relationship.

supportedRelationships.name: success
supportedRelationships.description: Any FlowFile that is successfully fetched from the file system will be transferred to this Relationship.

processors.typeDescription: Reads the contents of a file from disk and streams it into the contents of an incoming FlowFile. Once this is done, the file is optionally moved elsewhere or deleted to help keep the file system organized.

Endpoint Override URL.name: Endpoint Override URL
Endpoint Override URL.description: Overrides the default Google Cloud Storage endpoints

GCP Credentials Provider Service.name: GCP Credentials Provider Service
GCP Credentials Provider Service.description: The Controller Service used to obtain Google Cloud Platform credentials. Should be the name of a GCPCredentialsControllerService.

Number of retries.name: Number of retries
Number of retries.description: How many retry attempts should be made before routing to the failure relationship.

Object Generation.name: Object Generation
Object Generation.description: The generation of the Object to download. If left empty, then it will download the latest generation.

Proxy Configuration Service.name: Proxy Configuration Service
Proxy Configuration Service.description: Specifies the Proxy Configuration Controller Service to proxy network requests.

Server Side Encryption Key.name: Server Side Encryption Key
Server Side Encryption Key.description: The AES256 Encryption Key (encoded in base64) for server-side decryption of the object.

supportedRelationships.name: failure
supportedRelationships.description: FlowFiles are routed to this relationship if the Google Cloud Storage operation fails.

supportedRelationships.name: success
supportedRelationships.description: FlowFiles are routed to this relationship after a successful Google Cloud Storage operation.

processors.typeDescription: Fetches a file from a Google Cloud Bucket. Designed to be used in tandem with ListGCSBucket.

Connection Per FlowFile.name: Connection Per FlowFile
Connection Per FlowFile.description: Specifies whether to send each FlowFile's content on an individual connection.

Hostname.name: Hostname
Hostname.description: The ip address or hostname of the destination.

Idle Connection Expiration.name: Idle Connection Expiration
Idle Connection Expiration.description: The amount of time a connection should be held open without being used before closing the connection. A value of 0 seconds will disable this feature.

Port.name: Port
Port.description: The port or service on the destination.

Record Set Writer.name: Record Set Writer
Record Set Writer.description: Specifies the Controller Service to use for writing results to a FlowFile.

SSL Context Service.name: SSL Context Service
SSL Context Service.description: The Controller Service to use in order to obtain an SSL Context. If this property is set, messages will be sent over a secure connection.

Timeout.name: Timeout
Timeout.description: The timeout for connecting to and communicating with the destination.

supportedRelationships.name: failure
supportedRelationships.description: An error occurred processing

supportedRelationships.name: success
supportedRelationships.description: Successfully processed

processors.typeDescription: Processor able to read data from industrial PLCs using Modbus TCP/IP

Application URI.name: Application URI
Application URI.description: Application URI of the client in the format 'urn:unconfigured:application'. Mandatory, if using Secure Channel and must match the URI included in the certificate's Subject Alternative Names.

Certificate path.name: Certificate path
Certificate path.description: Path to the DER-encoded cert file

Key path.name: Key path
Key path.description: Path to the DER-encoded key file

Lazy mode.name: Lazy mode
Lazy mode.description: Only creates flowfiles from nodes with new timestamp from the server. If set to 'New Value', it will only create flowfiles if the value of the node data has changed since the last fetch, the timestamp is ignored.

Max depth.name: Max depth
Max depth.description: Specifiec the max depth of browsing. 0 means unlimited.

Node ID.name: Node ID
Node ID.description: Specifies the ID of the root node to traverse. In case of a Path Node ID Type, the path should be provided in the format of 'path/to/node'.

Node ID type.name: Node ID type
Node ID type.description: Specifies the type of the provided node ID

OPC server endpoint.name: OPC server endpoint
OPC server endpoint.description: Specifies the address, port and relative path of an OPC endpoint

Path reference types.name: Path reference types
Path reference types.description: Specify the reference types between nodes in the path if Path Node ID type is used. If not provided, all reference types are assumed to be Organizes. The format is 'referenceType1/referenceType2/.../referenceTypeN' and the supported reference types are Organizes, HasComponent, HasProperty, and HasSubtype.

Trusted server certificate path.name: Trusted server certificate path
Trusted server certificate path.description: Comma separated list of paths to the DER-encoded trusted server certificates

supportedRelationships.name: failure
supportedRelationships.description: Retrieved OPC-UA nodes where value cannot be extracted (only if enabled)

supportedRelationships.name: success
supportedRelationships.description: Successfully retrieved OPC-UA nodes

processors.typeDescription: Fetches OPC-UA node

AWS Credentials Provider service.name: AWS Credentials Provider service
AWS Credentials Provider service.description: The name of the AWS Credentials Provider controller service that is used to obtain AWS credentials.

Communications Timeout.name: Communications Timeout
Communications Timeout.description: Sets the timeout of the communication between the AWS server and the client

Credentials File.name: Credentials File
Credentials File.description: Path to a file containing AWS access key and secret key in properties file format. Properties used: accessKey and secretKey

Endpoint Override URL.name: Endpoint Override URL
Endpoint Override URL.description: Endpoint URL to use instead of the AWS default including scheme, host, port, and path. The AWS libraries select an endpoint URL based on the AWS region, but this property overrides the selected endpoint URL, allowing use with other S3-compatible endpoints.

Object Key.name: Object Key
Object Key.description: The key of the S3 object. If none is given the filename attribute will be used by default.

Proxy Configuration Service.name: Proxy Configuration Service
Proxy Configuration Service.description: Specifies the Proxy Configuration Controller Service to proxy network requests. When used, this will override any values specified for Proxy Host, Proxy Port, Proxy Username, and Proxy Password properties.

Proxy Host.name: Proxy Host
Proxy Host.description: Proxy host name or IP. Use https:// prefix for HTTPS proxy. DEPRECATED, please use Proxy Configuration Service instead.

Proxy Password.name: Proxy Password
Proxy Password.description: Password to set when authenticating against proxy. DEPRECATED, please use Proxy Configuration Service instead.

Proxy Port.name: Proxy Port
Proxy Port.description: The port number of the proxy host. DEPRECATED, please use Proxy Configuration Service instead.

Proxy Username.name: Proxy Username
Proxy Username.description: Username to set when authenticating against proxy. DEPRECATED, please use Proxy Configuration Service instead.

Requester Pays.name: Requester Pays
Requester Pays.description: If true, indicates that the requester consents to pay any charges associated with retrieving objects from the S3 bucket. This sets the 'x-amz-request-payer' header to 'requester'.

Use Default Credentials.name: Use Default Credentials
Use Default Credentials.description: If true, uses the Default Credential chain, including EC2 instance profiles or roles, environment variables, default user credentials, etc.

Version.name: Version
Version.description: The Version of the Object to download

Completion Strategy.name: Completion Strategy
Completion Strategy.description: Specifies what to do with the original file on the server once it has been pulled into NiFi. If the Completion Strategy fails, a warning will be logged but the data will still be transferred.

Connection Timeout.name: Connection Timeout
Connection Timeout.description: Amount of time to wait before timing out while creating a connection

Create Directory.name: Create Directory
Create Directory.description: Specifies whether or not the remote directory should be created if it does not exist.

Data Timeout.name: Data Timeout
Data Timeout.description: When transferring a file between the local and remote system, this value specifies how long is allowed to elapse without any data being transferred between systems

Disable Directory Listing.name: Disable Directory Listing
Disable Directory Listing.description: Control how 'Move Destination Directory' is created when 'Completion Strategy' is 'Move File' and 'Create Directory' is enabled. If set to 'true', directory listing is not performed prior to create missing directories. By default, this processor executes a directory listing command to see target directory existence before creating missing directories. However, there are situations that you might need to disable the directory listing such as the following. Directory listing might fail with some permission setups (e.g. chmod 100) on a directory. Also, if any other SFTP client created the directory after this processor performed a listing and before a directory creation request by this processor is finished, then an error is returned because the directory already exists.

Host Key File.name: Host Key File
Host Key File.description: If supplied, the given file will be used as the Host Key; otherwise, no use host key file will be used

Hostname.name: Hostname
Hostname.description: The fully qualified hostname or IP address of the remote system

Move Destination Directory.name: Move Destination Directory
Move Destination Directory.description: The directory on the remote server to move the original file to once it has been ingested into NiFi. This property is ignored unless the Completion Strategy is set to 'Move File'. The specified directory must already exist on the remote system if 'Create Directory' is disabled, or the rename will fail.

Port.name: Port
Port.description: The port that the remote system is listening on for file transfers

Private Key Path.name: Private Key Path
Private Key Path.description: The fully qualified path to the Private Key file

Proxy Host.name: Proxy Host
Proxy Host.description: The fully qualified hostname or IP address of the proxy server

Proxy Port.name: Proxy Port
Proxy Port.description: The port of the proxy server

Proxy Type.name: Proxy Type
Proxy Type.description: Specifies the Proxy Configuration Controller Service to proxy network requests. If set, it supersedes proxy settings configured per component. Supported proxies: HTTP + AuthN, SOCKS + AuthN

Remote File.name: Remote File
Remote File.description: The fully qualified filename on the remote system

Send Keep Alive On Timeout.name: Send Keep Alive On Timeout
Send Keep Alive On Timeout.description: Indicates whether or not to send a single Keep Alive message when SSH socket times out

Strict Host Key Checking.name: Strict Host Key Checking
Strict Host Key Checking.description: Indicates whether or not strict enforcement of hosts keys should be applied

Use Compression.name: Use Compression
Use Compression.description: Indicates whether or not ZLIB compression should be used when transferring files

supportedRelationships.name: comms.failure
supportedRelationships.description: Any FlowFile that could not be fetched from the remote server due to a communications failure will be transferred to this Relationship.

supportedRelationships.name: not.found
supportedRelationships.description: Any FlowFile for which we receive a 'Not Found' message from the remote server will be transferred to this Relationship.

supportedRelationships.name: permission.denied

Path.name: Path

processors.typeDescription: Allows manipulation of entries within an archive (e.g. TAR) by focusing on one entry within the archive at a time. When an archive entry is focused, that entry is treated as the content of the FlowFile and may be manipulated independently of the rest of the archive. To restore the FlowFile to its original state, use UnfocusArchiveEntry.

Batch Size.name: Batch Size
Batch Size.description: The number of FlowFiles to be transferred in each invocation

Custom Text.name: Custom Text
Custom Text.description: If Data Format is text and if Unique FlowFiles is false, then this custom text will be used as content of the generated FlowFiles and the File Size will be ignored. Finally, if Expression Language is used, evaluation will be performed only once per batch of generated FlowFiles

Data Format.name: Data Format
Data Format.description: Specifies whether the data should be Text or Binary

File Size.name: File Size
File Size.description: The size of the file that will be used

Unique FlowFiles.name: Unique FlowFiles
Unique FlowFiles.description: If true, each FlowFile that is generated will be unique. If false, a random value will be generated and all FlowFiles will get the same content but this offers much higher throughput (but see the description of Custom Text for special non-random use cases)

Bucket Name.name: Bucket Name
Bucket Name.description: The name of bucket to access.

Collection Name.name: Collection Name
Collection Name.description: Collection to use inside the bucket scope. If not specified, the _default collection is used.

Couchbase Cluster Controller Service.name: Couchbase Cluster Controller Service
Couchbase Cluster Controller Service.description: A Couchbase Cluster Controller Service which manages connections to a Couchbase cluster.

Document Id.name: Document Id
Document Id.description: A static, fixed Couchbase document id, or an expression to construct the Couchbase document id.

Document Type.name: Document Type
Document Type.description: Content type of the retrieved value.

Put Value to Attribute.name: Put Value to Attribute
Put Value to Attribute.description: If set, the retrieved value will be put into an attribute of the FlowFile instead of a the content of the FlowFile. The attribute key to put to is determined by evaluating value of this property.

Scope Name.name: Scope Name
Scope Name.description: Scope to use inside the bucket. If not specified, the _default scope is used.

NOTE: if the Document Id property is not set, the contents of the FlowFile will be read to determine the Document Id, which means that the contents of the entire FlowFile will be buffered in memory.

Batch Size.name: Batch Size
Batch Size.description: The maximum number of files to pull in each iteration

File Filter.name: File Filter
File Filter.description: Only files whose names match the given regular expression will be picked up

Ignore Hidden Files.name: Ignore Hidden Files
Ignore Hidden Files.description: Indicates whether or not hidden files should be ignored

Input Directory.name: Input Directory
Input Directory.description: The input directory from which to pull files

Keep Source File.name: Keep Source File
Keep Source File.description: If true, the file is not deleted after it has been copied to the Content Repository

Maximum File Age.name: Maximum File Age
Maximum File Age.description: The maximum age that a file must be in order to be pulled; any file older than this amount of time (according to last modification date) will be ignored

Maximum File Size.name: Maximum File Size
Maximum File Size.description: The maximum size that a file can be in order to be pulled

Minimum File Age.name: Minimum File Age
Minimum File Age.description: The minimum age that a file must be in order to be pulled; any file younger than this amount of time (according to last modification date) will be ignored

Minimum File Size.name: Minimum File Size
Minimum File Size.description: The minimum size that a file can be in order to be pulled

Polling Interval.name: Polling Interval
Polling Interval.description: Indicates how long to wait before performing a directory listing

Recurse Subdirectories.name: Recurse Subdirectories
Recurse Subdirectories.description: Indicates whether or not to pull files from subdirectories

Endpoint List.name: Endpoint List
Endpoint List.description: A comma delimited list of the endpoints to connect to. The format should be <server_address>:<port>.

Max Batch Size.name: Max Batch Size
Max Batch Size.description: The maximum number of messages to process at a time.

Max Size of Message Queue.name: Max Size of Message Queue
Max Size of Message Queue.description: Maximum number of messages allowed to be buffered before processing them when the processor is triggered. If the buffer is full, the message is ignored. If set to zero the buffer is unlimited.

Maximum Message Size.name: Maximum Message Size
Maximum Message Size.description: Optional size of the buffer to receive data in.

Message Delimiter.name: Message Delimiter
Message Delimiter.description: Character that denotes the end of the message.

Reconnection Interval.name: Reconnection Interval
Reconnection Interval.description: The duration to wait before attempting to reconnect to the endpoints.

Timeout.name: Timeout
Timeout.description: The timeout for connecting to and communicating with the destination.

supportedRelationships.name: partial

Fail on empty.name: Fail on empty
Fail on empty.description: Route to failure relationship in case of empty content

Hash Algorithm.description: Name of the algorithm used to generate checksum

Always Output Response.name: Always Output Response
Always Output Response.description: Will force a response FlowFile to be generated and routed to the 'Response' relationship regardless of what the server status code received is

Attributes to Send.name: Attributes to Send
Attributes to Send.description: Regular expression that defines which attributes to send as HTTP headers in the request. If not defined, no attributes are sent as headers.

Connection Timeout.name: Connection Timeout
Connection Timeout.description: Max wait time for connection to remote service

Content-type.name: Content-type
Content-type.description: The Content-Type to specify for when content is being transmitted through a PUT, POST or PATCH. In the case of an empty value after evaluating an expression language expression, Content-Type defaults to

Disable Peer Verification.name: Disable Peer Verification
Disable Peer Verification.description: DEPRECATED. The value is ignored, peer and host verification are always performed when using SSL/TLS.

Download Speed Limit.name: Download Speed Limit
Download Speed Limit.description: Maximum download speed,e.g. '500 KB/s'. Leave this empty if you want no limit.

Follow Redirects.name: Follow Redirects
Follow Redirects.description: Follow HTTP redirects issued by remote server.

HTTP Method.name: HTTP Method
HTTP Method.description: HTTP request method. Methods other than POST, PUT and PATCH will be sent without a message body.

Include Date Header.name: Include Date Header
Include Date Header.description: Include an RFC-2616 Date header in the request.

Invalid HTTP Header Field Handling Strat.name: Invalid HTTP Header Field Handling Strategy
Invalid HTTP Header Field Handling Strat.description: Indicates what should happen when an attribute's name is not a valid HTTP header field name. Options: transform - invalid characters are replaced, fail - flow file is transferred to failure, drop - drops invalid attributes from HTTP message

Penalize on \"No Retry\".name: Penalize on "No Retry"
Penalize on \"No Retry\".description: Enabling this property will penalize FlowFiles that are routed to the "No Retry" relationship.

Proxy Host.name: Proxy Host
Proxy Host.description: The fully qualified hostname or IP address of the proxy server

Proxy Port.name: Proxy Port
Proxy Port.description: The port of the proxy server

Put Response Body in Attribute.name: Put Response Body in Attribute
Put Response Body in Attribute.description: If set, the response body received back will be put into an attribute of the original FlowFile instead of a separate FlowFile. The attribute key to put to is determined by evaluating value of this property.

Read Timeout.name: Read Timeout
Read Timeout.description: Max wait time for response from remote service

Remote URL.name: Remote URL
Remote URL.description: Remote URL which will be connected to, including scheme, host, port, path.

SSL Context Service.name: SSL Context Service
SSL Context Service.description: The SSL Context Service used to provide client certificate information for TLS/SSL (https) connections.

Send Message Body.name: Send Message Body
Send Message Body.description: If true, sends the HTTP message body on POST/PUT/PATCH requests (default). If false, suppresses the message body and content-type header for these requests.

Upload Speed Limit.name: Upload Speed Limit
Upload Speed Limit.description: Maximum upload speed, e.g. '500 KB/s'. Leave this empty if you want no limit.

Use Chunked Encoding.name: Use Chunked Encoding
Use Chunked Encoding.description: When POST'ing, PUT'ing or PATCH'ing content set this property to true in order to not pass the 'Content-length' header and instead send 'Transfer-Encoding' with a value of 'chunked'. This will enable the data transfer mechanism which was introduced in HTTP 1.1 to pass data of unknown lengths in chunks.

invokehttp-proxy-password.name: invokehttp-proxy-password
invokehttp-proxy-password.description: Password to set when authenticating against proxy

invokehttp-proxy-username.name: invokehttp-proxy-username
invokehttp-proxy-username.description: Username to set when authenticating against proxy

send-message-body.name: send-message-body
send-message-body.description: DEPRECATED. Only kept for backwards compatibility, no functionality is included.

processors.typeDescription: Starts an HTTP Server and listens on a given base path to transform incoming requests into FlowFiles. The default URI of the Service will be http://{hostname}:{port}/contentListener. Only HEAD, POST, and GET requests are supported. PUT, and DELETE will result in an error and the HTTP response status code 405. The response body text for all requests, by default, is empty (length of 0). A static response body can be set for a given URI by sending input files to ListenHTTP with the http.type attribute set to response_body. The response body FlowFile filename attribute is appended to the Base Path property (separated by a /) when mapped to incoming requests. The mime.type attribute of the response body FlowFile is used for the Content-type header in responses. Response body content can be cleared by sending an empty (size 0) FlowFile for a given URI mapping.

A listed entity is considered 'new/updated' and a FlowFile is emitted if one of following condition meets: 1. does not exist in the already-listed entities, 2. has newer timestamp than the cached entity, 3. has different size than the cached entity. If a cached entity's timestamp becomes older than specified time window, that entity will be removed from the cached already-listed entities. Used by 'Tracking Entities' strategy.

Debug contexts.description: A comma-separated list of debug contexts to enable.Including: generic, broker, topic, metadata, feature, queue, msg, protocol, cgrp, security, fetch, interceptor, plugin, consumer, admin, eos, all

Delivery Guarantee.name: Delivery Guarantee
Delivery Guarantee.description: Specifies the requirement for guaranteeing that a message is sent to Kafka. Valid values are 0 (do not wait for acks), -1 or all (block until message is committed by all in sync replicas) or any concrete number of nodes.

Fail empty flow files.name: Fail empty flow files
Fail empty flow files.description: Keep backwards compatibility with <=0.7.0 bug which caused flow files with empty content to not be published to Kafka and forwarded to failure. The old behavior is deprecated. Use connections to drop empty flow files!

Kafka Key.name: Kafka Key
Kafka Key.description: The key to use for the message. If not specified, the UUID of the flow file is used as the message key.

Kerberos Keytab Path.name: Kerberos Keytab Path
Kerberos Keytab Path.description: The path to the location on the local filesystem where the kerberos keytab is located. Read permission on the file is required.

Known Brokers.name: Known Brokers
Known Brokers.description: A comma-separated list of known Kafka Brokers in the format <host>:<port>

Max Flow Segment Size.name: Max Flow Segment Size
Max Flow Segment Size.description: Maximum flow content payload segment size for the kafka record. 0 B means unlimited.

Max Request Size.name: Max Request Size
Max Request Size.description: Maximum Kafka protocol request message size

Message Key Field.name: Message Key Field
Message Key Field.description: DEPRECATED, does not work -- use Kafka Key instead

Message Timeout.description: The total time sending a message could take

Queue Buffering Max Time.name: Queue Buffering Max Time
Queue Buffering Max Time.description: Delay to wait for messages in the producer queue to accumulate before constructing message batches

Queue Max Buffer Size.name: Queue Max Buffer Size
Queue Max Buffer Size.description: Maximum total message size sum allowed on the producer queue

Queue Max Message.name: Queue Max Message
Queue Max Message.description: Maximum number of messages allowed on the producer queue

Request Timeout.name: Request Timeout
Request Timeout.description: The ack timeout of the producer request

SASL Mechanism.description: The SASL mechanism to use for authentication. Corresponds to Kafka's 'sasl.mechanism' property.

Security Pass Phrase.description: DEPRECATED in favor of SSL Context Service.Private key passphrase

Security Private Key.name: Security Private Key

Security Protocol.name: Security Protocol
Security Protocol.description: Protocol used to communicate with brokers. Corresponds to Kafka's 'security.protocol' property.

Target Batch Payload Size.name: Target Batch Payload Size
Target Batch Payload Size.description: The target total payload size for a batch. 0 B means unlimited (Batch Size is still applied).

Username.name: Username
Username.description: The username when the SASL Mechanism is sasl_plaintext

Broker URI.description: The URI to use to connect to the MQTT broker

MQTT Version.description: The MQTT specification version when connecting to the broker.

Message Expiry Interval.name: Message Expiry Interval
Message Expiry Interval.description: Time while message is valid and will be forwarded by broker. MQTT 5.x only.

Quality of Service.name: Quality of Service
Quality of Service.description: The Quality of Service (QoS) of messages.

Record Reader.name: Record Reader

Keep Alive Time.name: Keep Alive Time
Keep Alive Time.description: The period after which a keepalive ping is sent on the transport. If not set, then the keep alive is disabled.

Keep Alive Timeout.description: The amount of time the sender of the keepalive ping waits for an acknowledgement. If it does not receive an acknowledgment within this time, it will close the connection. If not set, then the default value 20 seconds is used.

Log Line Batch Size.name: Log Line Batch Size
Log Line Batch Size.description: Number of log lines to send in a batch to Loki. If this property and Log Line Batch Wait are both unset, the log batch of the current trigger will be sent immediately.

Log Line Batch Wait.name: Log Line Batch Wait
Log Line Batch Wait.description: Time to wait before sending a log line batch to Grafana Loki, full or not. If this property and Log Line Batch Size are both unset, the log batch of the current trigger will be sent immediately.

Log Line Metadata Attributes.name: Log Line Metadata Attributes
Log Line Metadata Attributes.description: Comma separated list of attributes to be sent as log line metadata for a log line.

Max Batch Size.name: Max Batch Size
Max Batch Size.description: The maximum number of flow files to process at a time. If it is set to 0, all FlowFiles will be processed at once.

Max Pings Without Data.name: Max Pings Without Data
Max Pings Without Data.description: The maximum number of pings that can be sent when there is no data/header frame to be sent. gRPC Core will not continue sending pings if we run over the limit. Setting it to 0 allows sending pings without such a restriction. If not set, then the default value 2 is used.

SSL Context Service.name: SSL Context Service
SSL Context Service.description: The SSL Context Service used to provide client certificate information for TLS/SSL (https) connections.

Stream Labels.name: Stream Labels
Stream Labels.description: Comma separated list of <key>=<value> labels to be sent as stream labels.

Url.name: Url

Azure Storage Credentials Service.description: Name of the Azure Storage Credentials Service used to retrieve the connection string from.

Blob.name: Blob
Blob.description: The filename of the blob. If left empty the filename attribute will be used by default.

Common Storage Account Endpoint Suffix.name: Common Storage Account Endpoint Suffix
Common Storage Account Endpoint Suffix.description: Storage accounts in public Azure always use a common FQDN suffix. Override this endpoint suffix with a different suffix in certain circumstances (like Azure Stack or non-public Azure regions).

Connection String.name: Connection String
Connection String.description: Connection string used to connect to Azure Storage service. This overrides all other set credential properties if Credential Configuration Strategy is set to From Properties.

Container Name.name: Container Name
Container Name.description: Name of the Azure Storage container. In case of PutAzureBlobStorage processor, container can be created if it does not exist.

Create Container.name: Create Container
Create Container.description: Specifies whether to check if the container exists and to automatically create it if it does not. Permission to list containers is required. If false, this check is not made, but the Put operation will fail if the container does not exist.

Credential Configuration Strategy.name: Credential Configuration Strategy
Credential Configuration Strategy.description: The strategy to use for credential configuration. If set to From Properties, the credentials are parsed from the SAS Token, Storage Account Key, and Connection String properties. In other cases, the selected Azure identity source is used.

Managed Identity Client ID.name: Managed Identity Client ID
Managed Identity Client ID.description: Client ID of the managed identity. The property is required when User Assigned Managed Identity is used for authentication and multiple user-assigned identities are added to the resource. It must be empty in case of System Assigned Managed Identity and can also be left empty if only one user-assigned identity is present.

Proxy Configuration Service.name: Proxy Configuration Service
Proxy Configuration Service.description: Specifies the Proxy Configuration Controller Service to proxy network requests.

SAS Token.name: SAS Token
SAS Token.description: Shared Access Signature token. Specify either SAS Token (recommended) or Storage Account Key together with Storage Account Name if Credential Configuration Strategy is set to From Properties. If set, Storage Account Key must be empty.

Storage Account Key.name: Storage Account Key
Storage Account Key.description: The storage account key. This is an admin-like password providing access to every container in this account. It is recommended one uses Shared Access Signature (SAS) token instead for fine-grained control with policies if Credential Configuration Strategy is set to From Properties. If set, SAS Token must be empty.

processors.typeDescription: Puts FlowFiles to an Amazon S3 Bucket. The upload uses either the PutS3Object method or the PutS3MultipartUpload method. The PutS3Object method sends the file in a single synchronous call, but it has a 5GB size limit. Larger files are sent using the PutS3MultipartUpload method. This multipart process saves state after each step so that a large upload can be resumed with minimal loss if the processor or cluster is stopped and restarted. A multipart upload consists of three steps: 1) initiate upload, 2) upload the parts, and 3) complete the upload. For multipart uploads, the processor saves state locally tracking the upload ID and parts uploaded, which must both be provided to complete the upload. The AWS libraries select an endpoint URL based on the AWS region, but this can be overridden with the 'Endpoint Override URL' property for use with other S3-compatible endpoints. The S3 API specifies that the maximum file size for a PutS3Object upload is 5GB. It also requires that parts in a multipart upload must be at least 5MB in size, except for the last part. These limits establish the bounds for the Multipart Upload Threshold and Part Size properties.

Connection Timeout.description: Amount of time to wait before timing out while creating a connection

Create Directory.name: Create Directory
Create Directory.description: Specifies whether or not the remote directory should be created if it does not exist.

Data Timeout.name: Data Timeout
Data Timeout.description: When transferring a file between the local and remote system, this value specifies how long is allowed to elapse without any data being transferred between systems

Disable Directory Listing.name: Disable Directory Listing
Disable Directory Listing.description: If set to 'true', directory listing is not performed prior to create missing directories. By default, this processor executes a directory listing command to see target directory existence before creating missing directories. However, there are situations that you might need to disable the directory listing such as the following. Directory listing might fail with some permission setups (e.g. chmod 100) on a directory. Also, if any other SFTP client created the directory after this processor performed a listing and before a directory creation request by this processor is finished, then an error is returned because the directory already exists.

Dot Rename.name: Dot Rename
Dot Rename.description: If true, then the filename of the sent file is prepended with a "." and then renamed back to the original once the file is completely sent. Otherwise, there is no rename. This property is ignored if the Temporary Filename property is set.

Host Key File.name: Host Key File
Host Key File.description: If supplied, the given file will be used as the Host Key; otherwise, no use host key file will be used

Hostname.name: Hostname
Hostname.description: The fully qualified hostname or IP address of the remote system

Last Modified Time.name: Last Modified Time
Last Modified Time.description: The lastModifiedTime to assign to the file after transferring it. If not set, the lastModifiedTime will not be changed. Format must be yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssZ. You may also use expression language such as ${file.lastModifiedTime}. If the value is invalid, the processor will not be invalid but will fail to change lastModifiedTime of the file.

Permissions.name: Permissions
Permissions.description: The permissions to assign to the file after transferring it. Format must be either UNIX rwxrwxrwx with a - in place of denied permissions (e.g. rw-r--r--) or an octal number (e.g. 644). If not set, the permissions will not be changed. You may also use expression language such as ${file.permissions}. If the value is invalid, the processor will not be invalid but will fail to change permissions of the file.

Port.name: Port

Private Key Path.name: Private Key Path
Private Key Path.description: The fully qualified path to the Private Key file

Proxy Host.name: Proxy Host
Proxy Host.description: The fully qualified hostname or IP address of the proxy server

Proxy Port.name: Proxy Port
Proxy Port.description: The port of the proxy server

Proxy Type.name: Proxy Type
Proxy Type.description: Specifies the Proxy Configuration Controller Service to proxy network requests. If set, it supersedes proxy settings configured per component. Supported proxies: HTTP + AuthN, SOCKS + AuthN

Reject Zero-Byte Files.name: Reject Zero-Byte Files
Reject Zero-Byte Files.description: Determines whether or not Zero-byte files should be rejected without attempting to transfer

Remote Group.name: Remote Group
Remote Group.description: Integer value representing the Group ID to set on the file after transferring it. If not set, the group will not be set. You may also use expression language such as ${file.group}. If the value is invalid, the processor will not be invalid but will fail to change the group of the file.

Remote Owner.name: Remote Owner
Remote Owner.description: Integer value representing the User ID to set on the file after transferring it. If not set, the owner will not be set. You may also use expression language such as ${file.owner}. If the value is invalid, the processor will not be invalid but will fail to change the owner of the file.

Remote Path.name: Remote Path

Send Keep Alive On Timeout.name: Send Keep Alive On Timeout
Send Keep Alive On Timeout.description: Indicates whether or not to send a single Keep Alive message when SSH socket times out

Strict Host Key Checking.name: Strict Host Key Checking
Strict Host Key Checking.description: Indicates whether or not strict enforcement of hosts keys should be applied

Temporary Filename.name: Temporary Filename
Temporary Filename.description: If set, the filename of the sent file will be equal to the value specified during the transfer and after successful completion will be renamed to the original filename. If this value is set, the Dot Rename property is ignored.

Use Compression.name: Use Compression
Use Compression.description: Indicates whether or not ZLIB compression should be used when transferring files

processors.typeDescription:
Sends the flow file contents to the specified Splunk HTTP Event Collector (see https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/SplunkCloud/latest/Data/UsetheHTTPEventCollector) over HTTP or HTTPS.

The "Source", "Source Type", "Host" and "Index" properties are optional and will be set by Splunk if unspecified. If set,
the default values will be overwritten with the user specified ones. For more details about the Splunk API, please visit
[this documentation](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/Splunk/LATEST/RESTREF/RESTinput#services.2Fcollector.2Fraw)

HTTP Event Collector (HEC) in Splunk provides the possibility of index acknowledgement, which can be used to monitor
the indexing status of the individual events. PutSplunkHTTP supports this feature by enriching the outgoing flow file
with the necessary information, making it possible for a later processor to poll the status based on. The necessary
information for this is stored within flow file attributes "splunk.acknowledgement.id" and "splunk.responded.at".

For more refined processing, flow files are enriched with additional information if possible. The information is stored
in the flow file attribute "splunk.status.code" or "splunk.response.code", depending on the success of the processing.
The attribute "splunk.status.code" is always filled when the Splunk API call is executed and contains the HTTP status code
of the response. In case the flow file transferred into "failure" relationship, the "splunk.response.code" might be
also filled, based on the Splunk response code.

SQL Statement.name: SQL Statement
SQL Statement.description: The SQL statement to execute. The statement can be empty, a constant value, or built from attributes using Expression Language. If this property is specified, it will be used regardless of the content of incoming flowfiles. If this property is empty, the content of the incoming flow file is expected to contain a valid SQL statement, to be issued by the processor to the database.

supportedRelationships.name: failure

supportedRelationships.name: success
supportedRelationships.description: After a successful SQL update operation, the incoming FlowFile sent here

processors.typeDescription: Executes a SQL UPDATE or INSERT command. The content of an incoming FlowFile is expected to be the SQL command to execute. The SQL command may use the ? character to bind parameters. In this case, the parameters to use must exist as FlowFile attributes with the naming convention sql.args.N.type and sql.args.N.value, where N is a positive integer. The content of the FlowFile is expected to be in UTF-8 format.

Connection Per FlowFile.name: Connection Per FlowFile
Connection Per FlowFile.description: Specifies whether to send each FlowFile's content on an individual connection.

Hostname.name: Hostname

Idle Connection Expiration.name: Idle Connection Expiration
Idle Connection Expiration.description: The amount of time a connection should be held open without being used before closing the connection. A value of 0 seconds will disable this feature.

Max Size of Socket Send Buffer.name: Max Size of Socket Send Buffer
Max Size of Socket Send Buffer.description: The maximum size of the socket send buffer that should be used. This is a suggestion to the Operating System to indicate how big the socket buffer should be.

Outgoing Message Delimiter.name: Outgoing Message Delimiter
Outgoing Message Delimiter.description: Specifies the delimiter to use when sending messages out over the same TCP stream. The delimiter is appended to each FlowFile message that is transmitted over the stream so that the receiver can determine when one message ends and the next message begins. Users should ensure that the FlowFile content does not contain the delimiter character to avoid errors.

Port.name: Port

SSL Context Service.name: SSL Context Service
SSL Context Service.description: The Controller Service to use in order to obtain an SSL Context. If this property is set, messages will be sent over a secure connection.

processors.typeDescription: The PutTCP processor receives a FlowFile and transmits the FlowFile content over a TCP connection to the configured TCP server. By default, the FlowFiles are transmitted over the same TCP connection. To assist the TCP server with determining message boundaries, an optional "Outgoing Message Delimiter" string can be configured which is appended to the end of each FlowFiles content when it is transmitted over the TCP connection. An optional "Connection Per FlowFile" parameter can be specified to change the behaviour so that each FlowFiles content is transmitted over a single TCP connection which is closed after the FlowFile has been sent. Note: When using TLS 1.3 the processor can still route the flow file to success if the TLS handshake fails. This is due to TLS 1.3's faster handshake process which allows the message to be sent before we know the result of the TLS handshake.

Hostname.name: Hostname
Hostname.description: The ip address or hostname of the destination.

Port.name: Port
